meaning_english: The practice of restraining oneself from indulging in something, typically alcohol or sex.
An award or privilege granted as a special honor or as an acknowledgment of merit.
An official pardon for people who have been convicted of political offenses.
A state of disorder due to absence or non-recognition of authority.
A statement or proposition which is regarded as being established, accepted, or self-evidently true.
The act or offence of speaking sacrilegiously about God or sacred things.
Abstinence from marriage and sexual relations, typically for religious reasons.
To link things together in a chain or series.
Compulsory enlistment for state service, typically into the armed forces.
All the people in a country or area who are entitled to vote in an election.
A person devoted to sensual enjoyment, especially that derived from fine food and drink.
Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail; hard to please.
Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters.
A person who attacks settled beliefs or traditional institutions.
So good or unusual as to be impossible to copy.

meaning_kannada: ವಿಶಿಷ್ಟ ಪರಿಭಾಷೆ (ನಿರ್ದಿಷ್ಟ ವೃತ್ತಿಯವರಿಗೆ ಮಾತ್ರ ತಿಳಿಯುವ ಭಾಷೆ)
meaning_english: Special words or expressions used by a profession or group that are difficult for others to understand.
A person who dislikes humankind and avoids human society.
A person who has an excessive interest in or admiration of themselves.
A notice of a death, especially in a newspaper, typically including a brief biography.
To exclude someone from a society or group.
A person of obscure origin who has gained wealth, influence, or celebrity.
A person who collects or studies postage stamps.
The practice of taking someone else's work or ideas and passing them off as one's own.
The leading character or one of the major characters in a drama, movie, or novel.
The practice of exchanging things with others for mutual benefit.
Violation or misuse of what is regarded as sacred.
A person who walks in their sleep.
A person who never drinks alcohol.
Present, appearing, or found everywhere.
Able to adapt or be adapted to many different functions or activities.
